English: What altitude does potato grow in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nīkūndū kūrīkū kūraihu mūno kūrīa waru ikūraga būrūri-inīwa Kenya?

English: Potatoes are traditionally grown between 1500 to 3000 metres Above Sea Level(m.a.s.l).
Kikuyu: Waru ithinūkagio gatagatīka mita 1500 na 3000 igūrū wa iria ((m.a.s.l.).

English: However, some new varieties can still do well in altitude below 1500 m.a.s.l.
Kikuyu: O na kūrīūguo, mīthemba īmwe njerū no īkorūo na ūguni o na irīma-inīirīna igūrū rīa mita 1,500.

English: Which soil types favors potato growing in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nīmīthemba īrīkū ya tīīri īteithagia kūrīma waru būrūri-inīwa Kenya?

English: Ideal soils for potatoes vary within the counties but usually loam soils (silt loam; clay loam) with adequate organic content, well-draining to light soils) are suitable.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ūrīa mwega wa kūhanda waru ūkoragwo ūrīngūrani kūringana na kauntī, no kaingīnīmūgūnda wa tīīrī(cilt loam; clay loam) ūrīna indo cia kūigana cia kīīmwīrī, na ūkoragwo na mīhūmū mīega ya maaī.

English: Suitable soil pH ranges from 5.5-7.0. Ideal soil pH 5.8
Kikuyu: pH ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire ya tĩĩri ĩkoragwo gatagatĩ ka 5.5-7.0. pH ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire ya tĩĩri 5.8

English: What is the ideal temperature for potato to grow?
Kikuyu: Nīrīera rīrīkū rīega rīa gūkūria waru?

English: Potato do well or require a mean daily temperature range from 15-24( degrees Celcius)
Kikuyu: Waru nīciekaga wega na ūrugarīmwega kana nīībataraga rīera rīa o mūthenya rīa digrii 15-24°C

English: How much annual rainfall does potato require to grow?
Kikuyu: Nīmbura īigana atīa ībataranagia o mwaka nīguo waru īhote gūkūra?

English: Potato require a Mean Rainfall of 1200-1800mm
Kikuyu: Waru nīībataraga Mbura ya mūigana wa 1200-1800mm

English: When is the ideal time to plant potatoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nīhīndīīrīkū njega ya kūhanda waru Kenya?

English: The ideal planting time for potatoes in Kenya varies based on the region and altitude.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīrīa rīagīrīire rīa kūhanda waru būrūri-inīwa Kenya rītihaanaine kūringana na kūrīa irīna kūrīa irīkūraihu.

English: Generally, potatoes are planted at the onset of the rainy season.
Kikuyu: Kaingī, waru īhandwo kīambīrīria-inīkīa hīndīya mbura.

English: For many areas, this translates to planting between March and May.
Kikuyu: Kwa icigo nyingī, ūndū ūcio ūrehaga kūhanda gatagatī-inīka Machi na May.

English: However, it's essential to consider local climatic conditions, and planting can be adjusted accordingly to ensure the crop receives adequate moisture for successful growth.
Kikuyu: O na kūrīūguo, nīūndū wa bata gwīciria ūhoro wa rīera rīa kūu, na mīmera no īcenjio kūringana na rīo nīguo īkorūo na ūigananīru atīmbegū nīīgīaga na ūrugarīūrīa ūrabatarania nīguo īhote gūkūra wega.

English: Where do potato grow in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Waru ikūraga kū Kenya?

English:
There are 13 major potato-producing counties which are: Meru,
Nyeri, Nyandarua, Kiambu, Taita-Taveta, Nakuru, Narok, Bomet, Elgeyo-Marakwet,
Trans-Nzoia, Bungoma, Uasin- Gishu and West Pokot
Kikuyu: "Kūrīkaunti 13 nene iria ikūragia waru ta Meru, Nyeri, Nyandarua, Kiambu, Taita-Taveta, Nakuru, Narok, Bomet, Elgeyo-Marakwet Trans-Nzoia, Bungoma, Uasin-Gishu na West Pokot"

English:
Other potato producing counties
include Kisii, Nyamira, Kirinyaga, Muranga, Baringo, Nandi, Laikipia and Kericho
Kikuyu: Kaunti iria ingīikūragia waru nīhamwe na Kisii, Nyamira, Kirinyaga, Muranga, Baringo, Nandi, Laikipia na Kericho"

English:
Upcoming potato producing counties include: Machakos, Makueni, Embu, Kajiado,
Tharaka Nithi, Samburu, Kwale and Nairobi
Kikuyu:
Kaunti īrīa īngīireukiriria gūkūria waru nī: Machakos, Makueni, Embu, Kajiado,
Tharaka Nithi, Samburu, Kwale na Nairobi"

English: Why is field sanitation in potato production?
Kikuyu: Nīkīīgītūmaga ūrigitani ūkorūo ūrīwa bata harīkūrīma waru?

English: Field sanitation prevents diseases and is done by the removal of all early disease inoculum in field before planting
Kikuyu: ūgītheria wa mīgūnda nīūgiragia mīrimū na ūrutagwo na njīra ya kweheria indo ciothe iria irīmūgūnda mbere ya kūhanda

English: potato plant residues, volunteer plants, culled tuber piles, residual tubers in soil, weeds
Kikuyu: matigari ma mīmera ya waru, mīmera ya kwīrutīra,mīrigo ya mbūrū, mĩmera ĩrĩa ĩtigarĩte tĩĩri-inĩ, mĩmera mĩũru

English: How is equipment sanitation ensured?
Kikuyu: Ũtethio wa ithui ni ũgwatĩkīrwo atĩa?

English: Equipment hygiene – clean all tools / tractors / shoes when going from one field to the next. ger crop.
Kikuyu: Gũthambia indo cia wĩra ⁇ gũthambia indo ciothe cia wĩra / macini cia gũkururia / iratu cia gũthiĩ kuuma mũgũnda ũmwe nginya ũngĩ.

English: In the morning start clean – in the newer fields and access older fields last – to avoid moving the inoculum from the older crop to the youn
Kikuyu: Rūcinīkwambīrīria gūtheria ⁇ mīgūnda-inīīrīa mīerū na kūingīra mīgūnda-inīīrīa mīkūrū mūthia  nīguo gwīthema gūthamia inoculum kuuma mūgūnda-inīūrīa mūkūrū nginya ūrīa mwerū

English: Which is the most recommended mode of irrigation in potato production?
Kikuyu: Nīnjīra īrīkū ya kūhehenja īrīa yagīrīire mūno harīkūrīma waru?

English: Overhead irrigation on hilled potato furrows is the most common irrigation way.
Kikuyu: Gūitīīrīrio maaīna igūrū nīnjīra īrīa īhūthagīrūo mūno ya kūitīīrīrio maaī.

English: This requires high water volume and a high-pressure pump to execute.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūcio ūbataraga maaīmaingīna bomu ya kūhūūra maaīna hinya mūnene.

English: Drip irrigation is the fastest growing method of irrigating potatoes because it is water efficient
Kikuyu: Kũminjamũra na njĩra ya kũminjamũra nĩ njĩra ya gũkũra na ihenya mũno ya kũhe waru maĩ tondũ nĩ ĩteithagia kũhũthĩra maĩ wega

English: When is irrigation most prefered in potato production?
Kikuyu: Nĩ hĩndĩ ĩrĩkũ kũhehenja kũhũthagĩrũo mũno mũgũnda-inĩ wa mahũa?

English: During tuber formation, irrigation is done sufficiently to support the crop’s increased water needs.
Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ya kũmbwo kwa mĩmera, mũgũnda ũhehagwo maĩ wega nĩguo ũhote kũhe irio iria ciendagio.

English: It is the most important stage of the crop irrigation.
Kikuyu: Nīyo ikinya rīrīa rīa bata mūno harīkūhe irio maaī.

English: When the tubers mature, less irrigation is carried out.
Kikuyu: Hīndīīrīa mīmera īyo īrerete, ndūhūthagīra maaīmaingī.

English: What is the best manure for potatoes?
Kikuyu: Nīthūmū ūrīkū mwega wa waru?

English: It has been shown that chicken manure pellets are the go-to choice fertilizer for potato farmers.
Kikuyu: Nīkuonekete atīmagego ma mūtararīko wa ngūkū nīmo magego marīa mathuuragwo mūno harĩ arĩmi a waru.

English: Potatoes thrive in calcium-rich soil, this helps produce large full flavour potatoes.
Kikuyu: Waru ciongagīra tīīri-inīwīna calcium nyingī, na ūndū ūcio nīūteithagia kūruta waru nene cia mūtararīko.

English: Chicken manure pellets contain up to 7% calcium.
Kikuyu: Mbūri cia ngūkū nīikoragwo na calcium ya hakuhī7%.

English: These pellets also release their nutrients slowly over 3 months.
Kikuyu: O na ningīmīmera īyo īkoragwo na irio nyingīīrekagiriria kahora ihinda-inīrīa mīeri 3.

English: Which fertilizers and manure are best for potatoes?
Kikuyu: Ni batalizer na mũtararĩko ũrĩa wega mũno harĩ waru?

English: Apply DAP fertilizer in the rows at planting at the rate of 500 kg/ha (or 200 kg/īka)
Kikuyu: Hũthĩra DAP mũtaratara-inĩ wa kũhanda na ũigana wa 500 kg/ha (kana 200 kg/īka)

English: On acidic soils, apply Monoammonium Phosphate (MAP) at the same rate as DAP
Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa tĩĩri wina acidi, ũhũthagĩre Monoammonium Phosphate (MAP) na ũigana wa DAP

English: Take half litre container and fill it with the fertilizer and apply uniformly in 15m furrow.
Kikuyu: Oya gĩkombe kĩa nuthu ya rita ũiyũrie na mũtararĩko na ũhũthagĩrũo na njĩra ĩmwe na mĩtaro ya mita 15

English: Fertilizer is applied in the rows and mixed thoroughly in the soil.
Kikuyu: Baterlizer ĩhũthagĩrũo na njĩra ya kũiga mĩtaratara na ĩgatukanwo wega na tĩĩri.

English: Apply well decomposed manure at the rate of 5-10t/ ha. Well rotten manure helps to prevent the occurrence
Kikuyu: Hũthĩra mũtararĩko mwega ũigana wa 5-10t/ha. Mũtararĩko mwega wa indo cia mũgũnda nĩ ũteithagia kũgirĩrĩria ũndũ ũcio

English: What is the best fertilizer for potatoes?
Kikuyu: Nĩ baterlizer irĩkũ njega makīrīa cia waru?

English: Potatoes require different types of nutrients to thrive.
Kikuyu: Nīguo waru ikūre, nīibataraga irio cia mīthemba ngūrani.

English: For example, potatoes need nitrogen to boost growth and potassium to protect the tubers from disease attacks.
Kikuyu: Kwa ngerekano, waru nīibataraga nitrogen nīguo ikūre na potassium nīguo īgitīre mīmera īyo ndīgathūkio nīmīrimū.

English: Potatoes will also require calcium and sulfur. Calcium strengthens the cell walls, making the plant less susceptible to diseases.
Kikuyu: O na ningīmbembe nīibataraga calcium na sulphur. Calcium nīīgīaga na hinya thīinīwa tūhengereta, na ūndū ūcio ūgatūma mīmera īyo īkorūo ītarīūgwati-inīwa kūrūara.

English: Sulfur is essential because it produces amino acids and methionine, which increases yields.
Kikuyu: Sulfur nīya bata mūno tondū nīīkoragwo na amino acids na methionine, iria ciongereraga magetha.

English: You can apply Sarfi Sarvi fertilizer, Safi foliar, or safi Biochar when using organic fertiliser.
Kikuyu: No ũhũthĩre mũhu wa Sarfi Sarvi, Safi foliar, kana safi Biochar rĩrĩa ũrahũthĩra mũhu wa kĩĩmwĩrĩ.

English: Safi biochar helps the soil hold nutrients and prevent erosion.
Kikuyu: Safi biochar īteithagīrīria tīīri kūiga irio na gwīthema kūhūahūana.

English: If you have a water problem, use Safi biochar because it traps moisture, preventing water from evaporating.
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo ūrīna thīna wa kwaga maaī, hūthagīra Safi biochar tondū nīīnyitaga ūrugarī, īgītūma maaīmatikanyitwo nīrīera.

English: What are some of the common fertilizers used in potato production in Kenya
Kikuyu: Nĩ baterlizer irĩkũ ingĩ cia bata ĩhũthĩrĩtwo harĩ kũhanda waru bũrũri wa Kenya?

English: Some of the common fertilizer used at potato planting include NPK 17:17:17, DAP, Baraka®, Yara Power® and MEA-NPK® 16:8:26.
Kikuyu: Nyūngū imwe iria ihūthagīrūo mūno hīndīya kūhanda mbembe nīta NPK 17:17:17, DAP, Baraka®, Yara Power® na MEA-NPK® 16:8:26.

English: Where DAP is chosen as the planting fertilizer, K should be supplied from another source, because DAP does not contain K.
Kikuyu: Harīa DAP īthuurītwo īrīmūgunda wa kūhanda, K yagīrīirwo kūrutwo na kīhumo kīngī, tondū DAP ndīna K

English: Application of K through sulphate of potash (SOP) is preferred to potassium chloride (muriate of potash or MOP) as potatoes are sensitive to chloride (Cl-).
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra K kūgerera potash sulphate (SOP) nīwega gūkīra kūhūthīra potassium chloride (muriate of potash kana MOP) tondū waru nīciīriragīria chloride (Cl-).

English: Is top dressing important in potato production in Kenya ? What are the recommended practices?
Kikuyu: Hihi kūgūria mbura nĩ ũndũ wa bata kũhũra waru bũrũri-inĩ wa Kenya? Nĩ njĩra irĩkũ ciagĩrĩire kũhũthĩrũo?

English: During a first fertilizer top-dressing, apply one quarter teaspoon (0.5 g) of urea to each plant on the shoulder of the ridge and immediately cover with soil.
Kikuyu: Hīndīya kūhūūra mūhu mbere, hūthīra gīcunjīkīa gīcunjīgīa inya gīa gīkombe (0.5 g) kīa urea harīmīmera o yothe īrīa īrīmwena-inīwa rūthingo na ūhumbīre na tīīri o hīndīīyo.

English: The first top dressing should be done one month after planting.
Kikuyu: ūhoro wa mbere wa kūhumba no mūhaka ūhumbwo mweri ūmwe thutha wa kūhanda.

English: This translates to 10 to 20 days after the sprouts emerge from soil.
Kikuyu: Mahinda macio makoragwo marīkuuma thikū 10 nginya 20 thutha wa tūhuti tūu kūmerūka.

English: A second top-dressing applies an additional 0.5 g urea to each plant, placed on the shoulder of the ridge.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa kerīrīa kūhumba, o mūtīūhūthagīrūo 0,5 g ya urea īngī, īgatwarwo mwena ūrīa ūngīwa rūthingo.

English: It should be covered by soil immediately after application.
Kikuyu: Kīndū kīu kīagīrīire kūhumbwo na tīīri o na ihenya thutha wa kūhūthīrūo.

English: The second top dressing should be done at the flower bud-formation stage.
Kikuyu: Gūthondekwo gwa kerīkwagīrīire gwīkwo hīndīīrīa mahūa marambīrīirie kūgīa.

English: What are some of the secondary nutrients required for potato production?
Kikuyu: Nĩ indo irĩkũ ingĩ cia bata harĩ kũhanda waru?

English: Secondary nutrients, required in smaller amounts, include calcium (Ca), magnesium (Mg), and sulphur (S).
Kikuyu: Indo ingīcia kūheaga ūgima wa mwīrī, iria ciabataranagia na tūcunjītūnini, nīta calcium (Ca), magnesium (Mg), na sulphur (S).

English: Micronutrients include boron (Bo), copper (Cu), chlorine (Cl), iron (Fe), manganese (Mn), molybdenum (Mo), and zinc (Zn), which occur in very small amounts in both soils and plants, but their role is equally as important as the major or secondary nutrients.
Kikuyu: Micronutrients nīhamwe na boron (Bo), copper (Cu), chlorine (Cl), iron (Fe), manganese (Mn), molybdenum (Mo), na zinc (Zn), iria cionekaga na tūcunjītūnini mūno tīīri-inīna mīmera-inī, no itemi rīacio nīrīa bata o ta ma nutrients marīa manene kana ma sekondarī.

English: What are some of the micronutrients important in potato production?
Kikuyu: Nīindo irīkū iria irīmuoyo imwe irībata harīgūthondeka waru?

English: Certain micronutrients must be supplied for high potato yields in some soils.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īmwe nīībataraga kūheo indo cia kūigana nīguo waru ikorūo na magetha maingīmīgūnda-inīīmwe.

English: Zinc (Zn) and manganese (Mn) may be needed in calcareous alkaline soils.
Kikuyu: Zinc (Zn) na manganese (Mn) no ikorūo irīcia bata mūgūnda ūrītīīri calcareous alkaline

English: For example, the fungicide Mancozeb is a source of Zn, and copper (Cu).
Kikuyu: Kwa ngerekano, dawa ya kūniina mīmera ītagwo Mancozeb īkoragwo na Zn, na copper (Cu).

English: Copper may be needed in peat soils but is usually sufficient in most mineral soils.
Kikuyu: Thitima no ībataranie mūgūnda-inīwa peat, no kaingīnīīkinyaga mūgūnda-inīwa mineral.

English: Boron (B) may be needed where the soils are deficient in it.
Kikuyu: Boron (B) no ībataranie kūrīa tīīri ūtarīna boron.

English: Iron (Fe), chloride (Cl- ) and molybdenum (Mo) are generally not deficient in soils that are used for potato production.
Kikuyu: Thīinīwa mīgūnda īrīa īhūthagīrūo gūthondeka waru, indo ta icango (Fe), cloride (Cl- ) na molybdenum (Mo) itiūraga.

English: The most effective application method depends upon the micronutrient, soil conditions, and the point in the growing season at which a deficiency is observed.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa njega mūno ya kūhūthīra irio icio īhocetie harīūrīa irio icio irīna tūcunjītūnini twa irio, ūrīa tīīri ūhaana, na ihinda rīrīa rīna bata wa irio icio.

English: Which fertilizer is recommended for potato production?
Kikuyu: Nīmūhihano ūrīkū ūragīrīrio kūhūthīra hīndīya kūrīma waru?

English: The Best soil to use in potato production will depend on the soil/leaf analysis that is done periodically as per program
Kikuyu: Tĩri Ũrĩa Mwega Ũngĩhũthĩra mũgũnda-inĩ wa kũhanda mbembe nĩ ũgũkorũo wĩhocetie harĩ mũgũnda wa tĩĩri/mathangũ gũthuthuria ũrĩa gwĩkagwo mahinda kwa mahinda kũringana na mũbango

English: Why is irrigation important in irish potato production and when should it be done?
Kikuyu: Nĩkĩ kũheana maĩ nĩ kwa bata na ni rĩ twagĩrĩirũo nĩ gwĩka ũguo?

English: For a good and regular growth, the potato plant requires a sufficient water supply, especially when the stolons (offshoots of the main roots where the potatoes are attached) are developing and the number of tubers is established.
Kikuyu: Nĩguo mbegũ ĩhote gũkũra wega, nĩ ĩbataraga kũheo maĩ ma kũigana. makĩria hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mĩmera ya mĩri ĩrĩa mĩnene ĩhũthagĩrũo kũhũũra waru o na gũtuĩka nĩ gũkoragwo na mĩmera mĩingĩ, na mũigana wa mĩmera nĩ ũmenyekete.

English: When the soil is dry during this period fewer tubers will be created.
Kikuyu: Hīndīīrīa tīīri nīmūūmū, ihinda-inīrīu mīmera ītarīmīingīnīīgūciarūo.

English: The ultimate way is the use of drip irrigation, this way the plant has sufficient water all the time, no water is spoiled by evaporation and the soil condition stays good.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa njega mūno nīkūhūthīra njīra ya gūitīīrīria maaīna tūramu, na njīra īyo mīmera īkoragwo na maaīma kūigana hīndīciothe, gūtirīmaaīmathūkagio nīkūhūyūka na tīīri ūkoragwo ūrīmwega.

English: In some soils the use of sprinkler irrigation will compact the soil so oxygen becomes a problem and the potatoes grow slowly and harvesting becomes difficult.
Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa mĩgũnda ĩmwe kũhũthĩrũo maaĩ na njĩra ya gũitĩrĩria maaĩ nĩ gũgatũma tĩĩri ũingĩhe Kwoguo riera rĩgatuĩka thĩna na waru ikũraga kahora na gũgetha gũgatuĩka ũndũ mũritũ.

English: If you are able to manage your time well and it is practical, we recommend irrigation to be done late afternoon to avoid a lot of evaporation.
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo no ūhote kūhūthīra ihinda rīaku wega na no ūhote, twagīrīirūo nīkūminyagīria maaīhwaī-inīnīguo gūtigakorūo na rīera rīingī.

English: Why is it important to practice crop rotation in potato seed production?
Kikuyu: Nīkīnīūndū wa bata kūhūthīra mūgūnda wa gūcenjania mbeū hīndīya kūrīma waru?

English: Have a good crop-rotation, just once in four years potatoes on the same field, otherwise the number of nematodes will increase.
Kikuyu: Gĩa na ihinda rĩega rĩa kũrĩma, o rĩmwe tu thutha wa mĩaka ĩna, mbembe ikoragwo mũgũnda-inĩ ũmwe, ĩngĩkorũo ti ũguo mũigana wa nematode nĩkwongerereka.

English: Fields for ware production should be free from nematodes!
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda īrīa īhūthagīrūo gūthondeka indo cia kūruga yagīrīirūo gūkorūo ītarīna mītarakwa!

English: If no other alternatives, use a nematicide (nematode killing or disabling chemical) before or simultaneously at planting.
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo gūtirīnjīra īngī, hūthīra dawa ya kūniina mītarakwa (chemical ya kūūraga kana kūniina mītarakwa) mbere ya kūhanda kana hīndīo īyo.

English: The same principle of crop rotation applies to the risk of bacterial wilt, which is becoming a big challenge in many parts of the country.
Kikuyu: ūndū o ta ūcio wa kūgayūkania mīgūnda ūhūthīkaga harīūgwati wa mīmera kūbutha, ūndū ūrīa ūrathiīna mbere gūkorūo ūrīūritū mūno icigo-inīnyingībūrūri-inī.

English: Once you have bacterial wilt in your farm, you will hardly get rid of it and experience significant decrease in yields plus a hardly sellable end product.
Kikuyu: ūngīgīa na bakteria mūgūnda-inīwaku, no ūkorūo ūrīūndū mūritū kūmīthūrima na ūgaakorūo na ūnyinyi mūnene wa magetha o hamwe na indo itangīendio.

English: The fields from which you will harvest your potatoes, can be planted with other crops that are not botanically related to potatoes.
Kikuyu: Mīgūnda īrīa ūkūgetha waru yaku no īhandwo na mīmera īngīītarīya waru.

English: Potatoes are in the Solanacea family, other crops in this family are e.g. tomatoes, capsicums, nightshade (managu), tobacco, eggplant, but also weeds like sodom apple.
Kikuyu: Ngarīki nīcia famīlīya Solanacea, na mīmera īngīthīinīwa famīlīīyo nīta Nyanya, capsicums, nightshade (managu), thigara, eggplant, na mīmera īngīta apple ya sodom.

English: Cereals and legumes are the best to rotate with potatoes for better pest/disease cycle disruption.
Kikuyu: Mahūa na matu nĩ njega gũcenjania na waru nĩgetha kũgĩe na mũgarũri mwega wa mĩrimũ na tũgunyũ.

English: What are the essential nutrients in irish potato production?
Kikuyu: Nĩ irio irĩkũ cia bata thĩinĩ wa gũkũra waru wa Irish?

English: Nitrogen: Nitrogen is important in fueling growth and providing high yields.
Kikuyu: Nitrogen: Nitrogen nīya bata harīkūhe irio irio na kūgīa na magetha maingī.

English: It ensures optimal photosynthate production in leaves.
Kikuyu: Nĩ ĩteithagia gũthondeka photosynthate wega mathangũ-inĩ.

English: Nitrogen is important in fuelling growth and providing high yields.
Kikuyu: Nitrogen nīya bata harīgūtwarithia na mbere wongerereku na kūhe magetha maingī.

English: It is largely needed during leaf formation and then for tuber growth and yield, when it ensures optimal photosynthate production in the leaves.
Kikuyu: Nĩ ũbataranagia mũno hĩndĩ ya gũthondeka mathangũ na thutha-inĩ nĩ ũndũ wa gũkũra kwa mĩmera na magetha mayo, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ĩgũthiĩ na mbere kũruta photosynthate wega thĩinĩ wa mathangũ.

English: Phosphorus: Phosphorus is important for early root and shoot development, providing energy for plant processes such as ion uptake and transport.
Kikuyu: Phosphorus nĩ ya bata harĩ Kũgĩa na mĩri na mĩmera ya mbere, kũheaga mĩmera hinya wa kũhũthĩra maũndũ-inĩ ta gũthondeka ioni na gũcitwara.

English: At tuber initiation, an adequate supply of phosphorus ensures supplies of optimum numbers of tubers are formed.
Kikuyu: Hīndīīrīa mīmera īramerūo, phosphorus īrīa yagīrīire nīīkoragwo na ūhotekeku wa kūiga mūigana ūrīa wagīrīire wa mīmera.

English: While potatoes are very responsive to fresh soil phosphate, the economic optimum rate is often very difficult to define.
Kikuyu: O na gūtuīka waru nīciiguaga wega rīrīa irīna phosphate mūgūnda-inīmwerū, kaingīti ūndū mūhūthū gūtaarīria ūritū wa phosphate ūrīa wagīrīire.

English: Rates will depend on soil type and soil test results.
Kikuyu: Mūigana wa indo icio ūkoragwo wīhocetie harīmūthemba wa tīīri na moimīrīro ma ūthuthuria wa tīīri.

English: Potassium: Potassium plays a key role in water relations and good overall plant health.
Kikuyu: Potassium: Potassium nīīkoragwo na itemi rīa bata harīkūnyua maaīna ūgima mwega wa mīmera.

English: Potato plants absorb large quantities of potassium throughout the growing season.
Kikuyu: Mīmera ya waru nīīnyitaga potassium nyingīihinda-inīrīothe rīa gūkūra.

English: Potassium has an important role in the control of the plant water status and ionic concentrations inside plant tissues, including stomata.
Kikuyu: Potassium nīīkoragwo na itemi rīa bata harīkūrūgamia ūrīa maaīma mīmera matariīna harīkūrūgamia ioni thīinīwa mīmera, o hamwe na tūnua twa mīmera.

English: As a result of the improved cell strength that potassium provides in potatoes, stress such as frost can be better tolerated.
Kikuyu: Nīūndū wa hinya ūrīa potassium īheaga tūhengereta twa mbatatī, mūndū no ahote gūkirīrīria maūndū moru ta heho.

English: Calcium: Calcium is a key component of cell walls, helping to build a strong structure and ensuring cell stability.
Kikuyu: Calcium: Calcium nīkīndū kīa bata mūno thīinīwa tūhengereta, na nīīteithagia gūthondeka tūhengereta tūrīa tūrīna hinya na tūkarūgitīra.

English: Internal disorders such as IRS (internal rust spot or internal brown spot - IBS) can be reduced by a good calcium supply, in the form of calcium nitrate, at ridging or during tuber initiation.
Kikuyu: Mathĩna ma thĩinĩ ta IRS (kĩronda kĩa thĩinĩ kana kĩronda kĩa thĩinĩ kĩa rangi wa burauni - IBS) no ĩnyihanyihio na njĩra ya kũheo calcium nyingĩ ta calcium nitrate hĩndĩ ya kũhanda kana hĩndĩ ya kũhanda mĩmera.

English: Experience shows that ensuring there is a minimum of 0.15% calcium in the peel, improves potato skin finish, boosts disease tolerance and minimizes IRS.
Kikuyu: Ũmenyeru wonanagia atĩ gũtigĩrĩra atĩ calcium nĩ ĩkoragwo na icunjĩ cia 0.15% thĩinĩ wa gĩkoro, nĩ ĩteithagia waru kūgia ngothi wega, ĩgatũma ĩhote kũhiũrania na mĩrimũ na ĩkaregana na mũrimũ wa IRS.

English: All nutrients,especially the major ones being N, P, and K, should be added in good balance as per your soil analysis, to avoid irregular feeding.
Kikuyu: Maciaro mothe, na makĩria marĩa manene N, P, na K, magĩrĩirũo gũcokanĩrĩrio wega kũringana na ũthuthuria waku wa tĩĩri, nĩguo gwĩthema kũrĩithia na njĩra ĩtarĩ ya kĩhooto.

English: Irregular feeding can lead to tuber cracks and hollow hearts, just like may happen if moisture is irregular during the growing season.
Kikuyu: Kũrĩa na njĩra ĩtarĩ ya kĩhooto no gũtũme mĩgogo ya tũcungwa na tũcungwa twa ngoro o ta ũrĩa kũngĩthiĩ angĩkorũo rĩera rĩa maĩ rĩtiagĩrĩire hĩndĩ ya gũkũra.

English: Calcium, Magnesium, Sulphur, and Borium are considered as part of the most secondary essential nutrients, just like NPK.
Kikuyu: Calcium, Magnesium, Sulphur, and Borium niciuíkanite ta irio  cia bata ota NPK

English: Why is ridging important and how and when should it be done?
Kikuyu: Kwenja mítaro ní gwa kíguni kíríkú na íbatie kwenjuo atiabna rí?

English: Ridging or hilling up the soil around the potato plant, helps the farmer in many ways.
Kikuyu: Gúthikíkíríra ní kwa bata kúrí múrímí harí njíra ta.

English: One is controlling weeds.
Kikuyu: Wambere, ní kúgiríríria gúthegea kwa ria.

English: The best moment to re-ridge is when the new plant is just emerging, at the 4-8 leave stage.
Kikuyu: Ihinda riega ria gúthikíkíríra ní riria waru ciaku ní ríría írutite mathangū 4-8

English: If there are weeds at this stage, you can cover them together with the potato plant, and they will not be strong enough to re-emerge, contrary to your potato plant.
Kikuyu: Angíkoruo ní harí ria ihinda rírí, ríkunike níguo ríremuo kumíra na hinya

English: A second reason for re-ridging is to give the new tubers more space to grow.
Kikuyu: Bata úngí wa gúthikíkíríra waru ní gúcihe kamweke kega ga gúkúra.

English: A big ridge also protects your tubers from growing out of the ridge onto the surface area, and as a result becoming green because of exposure to sunlight.
Kikuyu: Gúthikíkíríra ní gúteithagia waru citikoimire igúrú wa tírí naguo citigacenjie rangi wao nginya ngirini níundu wa ríua

English: Green tubers are not suitable for consumption, they are poisonous!
Kikuyu: Waru cia ngirini citibatie kúrio tondú ní thumu

English: Manual (re)ridging can be done with a jembe or hoe, where you dig some loose soil from the furrow and put it on top of the ridge.
Kikuyu: No úhúthire icembe,húma úgíthikíríra.  Ongerers tírí múhúthú kuma mútaro ini uongerere gítína íní kía waru.

English: The soil you heap to form a ridge also plays an important role to protect the young roots and stolons from the scorching sun when there are no rains.
Kikuyu: Oúgúo gúthikíkíríra ní gúteithagia kúmenyerera mírí mínini kúmana na riua ríría gútarí mbura

English: The trenches between ridges helps in harvesting the water during the rains, thus keeping your soil most until another rainfall.
Kikuyu: Mítaro íría ígatagati ka waru nííteitgagia kúiga maí hīndī ya mbura naguo tírí úgaikara na úgunyu nginyagia hīndī íngí ya mbura

English: Always be careful not to cut the stolons and roots when digging up the soil for ridging;
Kikuyu: Menyereraga úgíthikíríra ndúgathúkíe mírí

English: they grow very close to the surface and if you damage them a lower yield will be the result.
Kikuyu: Mírí íkúraga hakuhī na tírí na ingíthuka no ínyihie maciaro

English: Ridging / hilling up is making a nice wide and high ridge.
Kikuyu: Gúthikíkíríra nígúteithagia gúkara mikaro míarie

English: If the planting has been done properly just 2cms of soil will be added on top of the original ridge.
Kikuyu: Angíkoruo ní uhandite wega úbatie úongerere 2cm cia tírí igúrú ria tírí wa mbere

English: Never bury new plants deeper than 4cms, as this may stress or suffocate the plant and will bring an enormous decrease in yield.
Kikuyu: Ndúbatie gúthika múmera makíria ma 4cm tondú nocigie mímera yaku ya waru na úgúo kúnyihia magetha.

English: Do not ridge on very wet, or very dry, soils.
Kikuyu: Ndgakare kúndu kúhehu kana kúmú makíria

English: Moderate soil moisture is necessary for good firm ridges.
Kikuyu: Ūhehu wa gatagatíini ni mwega harí gúthondeka míkaro mínyitu

English: When using a tractor, you need narrow wheels to ensure you do not break or destroy the ridges with the tyres.
Kikuyu: itinga gúkara, tígíríra magúrú ní maceke níguo rítigathúkie mikaro íyo

English: What is the maturity period for irish potato in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Waru cíkúraga thutha wa kahinda kaigana atía Kenya?

English: Maturity Period: Range between 3 – 4 months after planting depending on the variety.
Kikuyu: Nigúo gúkúra warú cihúthagira mīeri 3-4 thutha wa kúhanda kúringana na mūthemba wa waru

English: Tubers harvested while still immature tend to have low dry matter content and to suffer more skin damage, resulting in easier infection by fungal and bacterial pathogens.
Kikuyu: Waru igethetuo citarī ngúrú ni úmíríria múnini na cigakoruo na thina wa ngothi na cikagwatia mírímú naihenya.

English: However, seed potatoes are often harvested early, to avoid virus infection that may occur during the latter part of the growing season
Kikuyu: Mbeu cia waru cigethaguo gatene níguo kúgiríríria itikanyitúo ní mírímú

English: What is the importance of dehaulming when and how should it be done?
Kikuyu: kúbuta mahuti ma waru marí bata úríkú na mabatíe kúrutuo atía?

English: Cutting vegetative material 2 weeks before harvesting hardens the skin of tubers.
Kikuyu: Kúrenga mathangú wiki igírí kabere gútumaga waru ciumírírie ngothi.

English: Hardening of skin tuber reduces damage of tubers during harvesting & post-harvest handling.
Kikuyu: Kúúmiríria ngothi íteithagia waru itigathúke cikígethuo na thutha wa kúgethwo.

English: What is the average irish production per īka in Kenya?
Kikuyu: magetha ma waru íka imwe maigana atía Kenya?

English: Average yield in Kenya: 3.2 tons/īka but the Yields potential: 16 tons/īka
Kikuyu: Magetha ma gatagatíini Kenya: ní tani 3.2 harí íka no írute tani 16 harí íka.

English: What is greening in potato and how can it be prevented?
Kikuyu: Waru kúgia rangi wa grini ní kuga atía na íngí girírírio atia?

English: The greening of potato is caused due to the exposure of the tubers to sunlight which causes the development of the green pigment called Solanin.
Kikuyu: Waru kúgia rangī wa ngirini níundu wa ríua. Rangi úyú uthondekaga kemiko ítaguo Solanin

English: This reduces the market value due to it's poor colour, taste and cooking quality.
Kikuyu: Rangī úyu níuteaga thoko níundu wa kwaga kúgucíríria, gútee múcamo na kwaga kúrugíka

English: This can be prevented by practicing earthing up after every top dressing.
Kikuyu: No úgírírírie úú úngíkíra tírí o thutha wa gúita bataraitha

English: This prevents the exposure of the tubers to sunlight.
Kikuyu: Gwíka úú kúgiragíríria waru citigacinuo ní ríua

English: Greening of potato is also usually seen in the tubers immediately after harvesting.
Kikuyu: waru no cigíaga rangī úyu thutha wa kúgetha níundu wa kwaníkúo ríua ini

English: This can be avoided by storing them in a cool and dark place
Kikuyu: Wítheme úú na kúiga warú handū hahehu na harí na nduma.

English: How can a farmer determine if irish potatoes are mature?
Kikuyu: Múrími angimenya atía waru ciake ní ngúru?

English: Maturity period varies from one variety to another and can be indicated by yellowing of the crop’s leaves and easy separation of tubers from their stolons.
Kikuyu: Gúkúra kwa waru kúringanaga na múthemba wa waru na umenyaga mahuti magia rangi wa ngoikoni

English: What is the recommended ph for irish potato production?
Kikuyu: Ni pH íríkú yendekithitio harí úkúria wa waru?

English: The recommended pH level for potatoes is 6.2.
Kikuyu: pH íría yendekithitío ni 6.2

English: However, from a soil with pH of 5.0 you can still grow potatoes, but then add lime to the soils.
Kikuyu: Harí o oúgúo ph ya kuma 5 no íkúrie waru no úcoke wongerere lime tírí íni

English: What is the recommended standard bag for harvesting potatoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: ní múhuko úriku wendekíthítio kúiga waru Kenya?

English: Potatoes are supposed to be sold in the standard 50kg units in the markets
Kikuyu: Waru cibatíe kwendio míhuko-ino ya kilo 50 thoko-ini

English: What are the factors to consider during harvesting of potato?
Kikuyu: Ní maundu maríkú ubatie kúrora úkígetha waru?

English: Harvest when the soil is dry;
Kikuyu: Getha ríría tírí wí mūmū

English: Do not expose harvested tubers to sunlight for a long time to prevent them from drying out too quickly and avoid greening of the white skin varieties, which would reduce their keeping quality and consumer acceptance.
Kikuyu: Dukaciaríre waru ciaku ríuaní kahinda karaihu niguo citikome narua na citikagie rangī wa ngirini kuma rangu mweru úria uri rangi wa míthemba mííngi ya waru. No cicenjie úgacíru na gúítíkíríka thoko-ini

English: Avoid harvesting when the soils are wet in order to avoid pathogens sticking on tubers.
Kikuyu: Witheme kugetha ríría kúri na úgunyu, níguo kúgiríríria kwínyitíríra kwa tútambi túria túngirehe mírímú.

English: How to manage potatoes while on the farm?
Kikuyu: Ungírora atía waru círí múgúnda ini?

English: One way to manage potatoes while on the farm is to start weeding at least 2 weeks after planting.
Kikuyu: Njĩra ĩmwe ya kũmenyerera waru rĩrĩa ũrĩ mũgũnda nĩ kwambĩrĩria kūmūnya ria ta ciumia igĩrĩ thutha wa kũhanda.

English: Failure to weed may result in poor yield because the weeds will compete for nutrients and moisture with potato tubers.
Kikuyu: úngíaga kúímíra waru ciaku, maciaro níkúnyiha manyihaga níundu wa gútharana irio na maí hamwe na waru.

English: Weeding can either be done manually or by the use of a herbicide.
Kikuyu: No urímíre na moko kana úhúre dawa ya ria

English: Test the type of herbicide they use on a small portion before applying it to the whole farm.
Kikuyu: Geria dawa ya ria handū hanini útanahúra múgúnda wothe

English: An agricultural expert should advise on the best application procedure.
Kikuyu: Buata mútaratara wa kúhúra dawa íno kuma kúrí abithaa a úrímí

English: What is the correct spacing to plant potatoes?
Kikuyu: Warú cíbatíe gútígíthíkanio atía?

English: Planting and spacing: For pure stand potato the spacing is 75cm between furrows , place tubers 30cm apart in the furrow.
Kikuyu: Kúhanda na gútigithúkania: muthemba wa pure stand, tígíthúkanía 75cm harí mítaro na harí waru 30cm mútaro íní

English: Avoid direct contact of the tubers with the fertilizer as this may have a burning effect on the tubers.
Kikuyu: Wítheme kúhutithania bataraitha na mbeu tondú bataraitha no icicine

English: Place tubers in furrows with the side having sprouts facing up.
Kikuyu: Íkira waru ciaku mítaro-íni na úreke mwena úria wina nguha úrore iguru

English: Cover the tubers with soil completely to avoid sun scorching
Kikuyu: Kuníka waru na tírí wega níguo citigacinuo ní ríua

English: What is the best time to plant potatoes?
Kikuyu: Ní ihinda ríríku ríega ria rīa kúhanda waru

English: Planting should be done shortly before onset of rains.
Kikuyu: Wagīrīruo kúhanda waru ciaku mbura íhakuhí kura

English: How to set up a potato farm?
Kikuyu: Múgúnda wa waru ūharagīrīrio atía

English: Before setting up a potato farm, you need to identify high-yielding varieties.
Kikuyu: Útanacagura múgúnda wa kúhanda,  úbatie wa mbere úmenye mútheba wa waru úría úciaraga múno

English: For example, the Sherekea variety produces 160 bags of potatoes per īka.
Kikuyu: Harí múhano, múthemba wa Sherekea no úgethuo míhuko 160 harí o íka

English: Farmers can also use Kenya Mpya, which produces 140 bags per īka.
Kikuyu: Arímí no mahúthíre Kenya mpya  íría íngígethwo míhuko 140 harí o íka

English: The seed variety you choose should be high-yielding, fast-maturing, and disease-resistant.
Kikuyu: Mbeu íría úgúcagúra ní íbatíe íkoruo na maciaro mega, írakúra na ihenya na gúítiria mírímú

English: How to prepare land for potato farming?
Kikuyu: Úharagírírírio atia  múgúnda wa waru?

English: The soil needs to be well cultivated, with a raised seedbed to ensure uniform and large potato tubers.
Kikuyu: Tírí ubatíe kúharírírio wega wíígananíire  na wina bed níguo waru ciaku cione ha gútamba wega

English: Also, organic matter in the ground that does not decompose quickly must be removed.
Kikuyu: Thumu úria uri tírí íní útarabutha úbatie kweherio naihenya.

English: This is because they may attract pests.
Kikuyu: Gītūmi ní atī níugucagíríria tútambi.

English: You should ensure that the land is plowed at least 20 cm deep and that there are no soil clods.
Kikuyu: Tígíríra níwaríma úriku útanyihíire 20cm na tírí ndūrī na magíkía

English: What is the correct way to plant potatoes?
Kikuyu: Ní njíra íríku njega njega ya kúhanda waru

English: It would be best if you planted the seeds at the onset of the rains.
Kikuyu: Ní wega úngíhanda mbura íríhakuhi kúra

English: When planting, plant in furrows.
Kikuyu: Úkíhanda rí, handa mítaro-íni

English: The plant should also be grown 30 cm from each other, with the sprouts facing up.
Kikuyu: Tígíthúkanía 30cm harí waru na úkíhanda rí, reke nguha irore igúrú.

English: The furrow depth should be between 8 -12 cm, allowing a tuber population of 18,000 per īka.
Kikuyu: Úriku wa mútaro úbatie gúkoruo 8cm-12cm

English: The spacing makes it easy for you to scout for pests and diseases, harvest, and spray the farm.
Kikuyu: Utígithúkanio úyú úteithagia híndí ya kúrora tútambi, mírímú, kúgetha na raithi kúhuhíra

English: It is also important to note that you should plant them in ridges with a height of 25 cm, making them easy to harvest.
Kikuyu: He bata makíría kúhanda míkaro-ini na útígithúkanu wa 25cm níguo gútigíríra ní úragetha raithi.

English: Also, the tubers have room for expansion.
Kikuyu: Waru ukona handū cingítunguhíra.

English: What is hilling, and why is it important in potato farming?
Kikuyu: Kúoera tírí níkíí na wí bata úríkú úrímini wa waru.

English: Hilling involves mounding soil around the base of growing potato plants.
Kikuyu: Gúthikíkíríra ní kuongerera tiri gítina ini kía waru

English: This practice is crucial for several reasons.
Kikuyu: Gwika úú ní gwa bata níundu wa maundû matiganíte.

English: It provides support to the plants, prevents greening of tubers by shielding them from sunlight, and encourages the development of additional tubers.
Kikuyu: Ní úteithagia múmera gwitíra, kúgítíra warú kúgía rangi wa grini níundu wa ríua, na gúteithia waru iciare makíria

English: Hilling also aids in weed control and improves soil aeration, contributing to overall crop health and a successful harvest.
Kikuyu: Makíría níitethagia kunyihia ria na kúreka tírí kúgía na ríera wega na úgúo múmera úgakúra wega na magetha makaingiha.

English: How many bags of potatoes can one īka produce in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Ní míhuko ígana atía íngígethuo kuma íka imwe Kenya?

English: Before setting up a potato farm, you need to identify high-yielding varieties.
Kikuyu: Útanacagura múgúnda wa kúhanda,  úbatie wa mbere úmenye mútheba wa waru úría úciaraga múno

English: For example, the Sherekea variety produces 160 bags of potatoes per īka.
Kikuyu: Harí múhano, múthemba wa Sherekea no úgethuo míhuko 160 harí o íka

English: Farmers can also use Kenya Mpya, which produces 140 bags per īka
Kikuyu: Arímí no mahúthíre Kenya mpya  íría íngígethwo míhuko 140 harí o íka

English: What is the profit of growing potato in one īka farm?
Kikuyu: Ní baita íígana atia harí íka thutha wa kúgetha waru

English: Potato profit per īka is between Kshs 90,000 – 200,000.
Kikuyu: Baita ya waru harí íka ni Ksh90,000_ 200,000

English: It is impossible to estimate the actual profit per īka because there are alot of factors involved.
Kikuyu: Ní harí hínya kúmenya baita harí o íka tondu ní maūndū maingí mahúthíkaga

English: One main factor that can determine the profit per īka is the variety.
Kikuyu: Ūndū ūmwe úngíteithia kúmenya baita harí o íka ní múthemba úría múhande wa waru.

English: For example, if you plant the Arizona variety, you can expect an average yield of 16 tonnes per īka.
Kikuyu: Harí múhiano, Angikúria mútheba wa Arizona wa waru no útanye kegetha tani 16 harí o mwaka.

English: What is the estimate for growing shangi variety?
Kikuyu: Thogora wa gúkúria warú wa shangi ní beca cigana hihi?

English: Shangi estimated potato farming costs are broken down as follows:
Kikuyu: Thogora wa Úkúria wa waru wa shangi ni ta úú:

English: Production costs: 46.500 KSH
Kikuyu: Thogora wa Úkúria: 46,500

English: Land preparation: 11,500 KSH
Kikuyu: Kúharíríria múgúnda: Ksh11,500

English: Fertilizer: 25,000 KSH
Kikuyu: Bataraitha :Ksh25,000

English: Fungicides and insecticides: 27,000 KSH
Kikuyu: Dawa cia tūtambi na fungi: Ksh27,000

English: Crop Management: 21,000 KSH
Kikuyu: Gūthaitīra mīmera: Ksh21,000

English: The cost can be higher or lower depending on several factors.
Kikuyu: Thogora no ūkorūo wī igūrū kana thī kūringana na maudu matīganīte.

English: For example, the prices can be higher if you include insurance costs.
Kikuyu: Kwa mūhano rī, thogora no ūkorūo wī igūrū hanini angīkoruo niūrarīhīra kaba ya Insurance.

English: The costs will also be higher if you lease land in urban areas.
Kikuyu: Thogora no ūkorūo ūri īgūrū ūngīkobora mūgūda taūninī.

English: How much is a 50kg bag of potatoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Thogora wa waru kilo 50 nib mbeca cigana?

English: A 50kg bag of potatoes retails for KSH 3,500, up from KSH 1,500 a month ago.
Kikuyu: Kilo 50 ya warū īrauma 3500 kuuma 1500 mweri mūhītūku.

English: That reflects a rise in prices per 50kg unit of KSH 2,000, with dealers expressing fears that a supply shortage may further push up the prices as year-end festivities start
Kikuyu: Ugūo nī kuuga atī ni ksh2000 kwabatīra gwa thogora,Ojorithia mena hahū tondū thogora ūyū no wabatīre twakuhīrīrīa maaburaīnī ma mathigūkū

English: How much is a bucket of potatoes in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Hihi baketi ya waru nī mbeca cigana Kenya

English: 10kg bucket at KSH 450/- 20kg bucket KSH 800/- 70/90 kg bag KSH 3,500/-
Kikuyu: 10kg nī ksh450, 20kg nī ksh800, mūhuko wa 70/90kg ni ksh3500

English: How to control late blight disease in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Mūrīmū wa baa kana heho ūgīragīrīrio atia?

English: The best way to control this disease is to use fungicides.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa njega makīria ya kugīrīrīa mūrīmū uyu ni kwaria na ataaramu a ūrīmī.

English: Its application should be based on the advice of an agricultural officer.
Kikuyu: Mītaratara ya urīmi wagūo ūbatiī kuma kūrī abītha a ūrīmī

English: In addition, spraying should only be done when the plants are at least 10 cm tall.
Kikuyu: Gūkīrī ouguo rī, kūhuhira mīmera kūbatiī gūkoruo mumera wakinyia 10cm

English: However, spraying should be done after symptoms are identified if the tuber variety is late blight disease resistant.
Kikuyu: O na kũrĩ ũguo,kũhũũra dawa nĩ wagĩrĩirũo nĩ gwĩkwo thutha wa kuonania atĩ kũrĩ na mĩrimũ wa late Blight

English: When spraying a fungicide, ensure you spray on the underside part of the leaves
Kikuyu: ūkīhuhīra fungicide tīgīrīrs urahuhīra mwena wathī wa mahuti

English: How to control early blight disease in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Mūrimu wa baa harī waru ungī īthemwo atīa?

English: Early blight disease can be controlled by prism crop rotation, using suitable seed varieties, rouging, and proper sanitation.
Kikuyu: Mũrimũ wa thithino no ũhote kũgirĩrĩrio na njĩra ya gũcenjia mĩmera, kũhũthĩra mbeũ cia mĩthemba ĩrĩa yagĩrĩire, na kũhũthĩra njĩra njega cia gũtheria.

English: In addition, farmers can also use systemic chemicals or contact fungicides to prevent the disease from spreading.
Kikuyu: Oūgūo rī, arīmi no mahūthīre njīra cia kīīriu cia kūgitīra guthegea kwa mīrimū

English: How to control bacterial wilt in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Mūrīmū wa bacteria wilt ungīgirīrīrio guthegea atia harī waru?

English: Though bacterial wilt has no known chemical control measure, farmers are encouraged to implement integrated disease management practices.
Kikuyu: onangīkorūo gūtīīrī dawa yūikaine ya kugīrīrīa gūthegea kwa bacteria wilt harī warū rī, arīmī nīmarahoywo, kūhūthīra njīra īrīa ciuīkaine cia kugīrirīa guthegea kwa mīrīmu

English: These practices involve planting healthy seeds, using disease-resistant varieties, and practicing crop rotation.
Kikuyu: Njīra īcī nī hamwe na, kuhada mbeu cīa mūthemba mwega irīa cīngītīria mīrīmū na kuhada mīmera ngūrani muganda o kīmera

English: How to control the black scurf disease in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Mūrīmū wa black scurf ungīgīrīrīrio atīa hari waru

English: One way to control the disease is to practice crop rotation, using non-host crops, using certified seeds, treating certified seeds before sprouting, and not planting in waterlogged soil.
Kikuyu: Njira imwe ya kūgīrīrīrīa mūrīmū ūyu ni hamwe na kuhada mīmera ngūrani mūgūda o kīmera, kuhuthīra mīmera atarī mīrwaru, kūhūthīra mbegū hetūkie, kūrīgīta mbegū hetūkie cītanaruta nguha na kwaga kūhada kūria tīri wina maaī maingī.

English: Farmers should not deep sprouted tubers in chemicals, as these can cause damage.
Kikuyu: Arīmī matibatīe kūrida nguha dawa īnī iria cīīna thūmu. Gwīka ūguo no gūtūme cithūke.

English: How to control blackleg and soft rot disease in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Njira cia kugīrirīria mīrīmu ya blackleg na soft rot harī waru nī īrīkū

English: The blackleg disease can be controlled using fungicides and by planting certified seed varieties.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū wa blackleg no ūgitīruo ūkīhuthīra dawa cīa fungicide na kūhada mbeū īrīa cīītīkīrītio

English: Farmers are also advised to be careful when weeding to avoid tuber injury.
Kikuyu: Arīmī nīmaratarūo kūmenyerera makīrīmīra nīgūo kūgīrīrīria gūthūkia mīrī.

English: Potatoes should also be grown using the correct spacing, and furrow irrigation should be avoided.
Kikuyu: Warū nīcībatiī gūkūrio wega ūkīrūmīrīra ūtīganu ūria ubatīe, gūkū ūgithema kurīma na maaī na njīra cia mītaro.

English: How to control mosaic virus in potatoes?
Kikuyu: Njīra ya kūgītira mūrīmū wa Mosaic vīrus nī īrīkū?

English: The best way to control the mosaic virus is to use good-quality seeds and rouging when there are symptoms.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa njega ya kugīrīrīria mūrimu wa Mosaic vīrus nī kūhūthira mbeu iria hetūkie, na gūcīthaitīra rīrīa darīrī cīa mūrimū uyu cīeyumīria

English: Also, potato farmers should remove and destroy affected plants.
Kikuyu: Harī ūguo rī, mūrīmi nī abatie kweheria mīmera īrīa īgūmīiruo nī mūrimū uyū

English: How to control aphids on a potato farm?
Kikuyu: ūme ungīgirīrīrio atia harī mūgūda wa waru?

English: The best way to control aphids is to practice proper field sanitation, such as good waste management and rouging.
Kikuyu: Njīra īrīa njega makīria yakugīrīrīa ūme nikūrora ūtherū wa mūgūda ta gūtigīrīra ūtei mwega wa gīko.

English: Farmers are also advised to use pesticides while wearing protective clothing.
Kikuyu: Arīmī nao nīmaratarūo kūhūthīra dawa cīa tūtambi na gūikīra ngūo īrīa ciramagītīra.

English: They are also encouraged to practice pre-sprouting to accelerate the growth of tubers.
Kikuyu: Orīngī nīmarataruo kūrida waru wao nīgūo kūhanyūkia kumera kwa nguha

English: How to control cutworms?
Kikuyu: īgūnyo cīa ngūnga cīngīnyihio atīa?

English: Cutworms can either be controlled by using systemic insecticides or contact insecticides.
Kikuyu: Ngunga cingīgirīrīo na dawa cīa tūtambī

English: Spraying should be done after you notice beetles or cutworms consuming foliage.
Kikuyu: Hūra dawa onarua ungīona nding'oing'o na ngūnga ciatharīkīra mīmera yaku.

English: Another pest that you need to know about is the potato tuber moth.
Kikuyu: Mutheba wa tūtambī ungī harī waru ni potato tuber moth.

English: This pest attacks potatoes while they are in the field and storage.
Kikuyu: Tūtambi tūtū tutharikagīra mīmera ī mugunda kana thitoo.

English: They create burrows through the stems exposing the plant to bacteria and viruses.
Kikuyu: īthegethaga mūkorīni na gutuma mūmera ūnyitūo nī bakterīa.

English: How to grade and sort potatoes?
Kikuyu: Warū cīthuragūo na kūgrīdūo atīa?

English: Before storing potato, misshapen, diseased and damaged tubers should be removed to avoid losses in storage due to rotting.
Kikuyu: ūtanaiga warū waku rī, thūra iria dwaru na thūkangie nīgūo kūgīrīrīria kūbūtha .

English: Potato are graded into three sizes depending on the diameter: Large or ware (greater than 55mm), medium (25-55mm) and smallest or chatts (less than 25mm).
Kikuyu: Warū cīthuragūo harī thaithī ithatu kurīngana na warīe: Nene(makīrīa ma 55mm), gatagatīini ( 25mm -55mm), nini ( thī wa 25mm)

English: Keep ware tubers in a cool dark well aerated store to prevent greening.
Kikuyu: Iga waru handū hahehu hena nduma, na rīera rīīngī nīgūo kūgīrīrīria gūcejia rangī na kūgīa wa grini.

English: Potato can be held in sacks for short periods (up to 1 month) but for longer periods, wooden crates are preferred.
Kikuyu: Waru no ciguo na makūnia gwa kahida kanini( Nginya mweri umwe) no harī kahinda karaya rī, īrītī cia mbaū ñī njega makīria na kūiga waru.

English: How can I protect my potato crop from pests and diseases?
Kikuyu: Ingīgīrīrīria atīa waru cīakwa kūmana na tūtambi na mīrīmū.

English: Implementing integrated pest management practices is key to safeguarding your potato crop.
Kikuyu: Kūhūthīra njīra cīa kūgītīra tūtambi ni njega makīrīa na kūmenyerera mīmera yaku.

English: Regular scouting for pests like aphids and Colorado potato beetles is crucial.
Kikuyu: Kūroora mūgūnda waku mahinda kwa mahinda nīgūo kumenya tūtambī ta ūme, Colorado na nding'oing'o cia waru he bata mūno

English: Utilize natural predators and organic pesticides to control pest populations.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra njīra na dawa cīa kīmerera kūnyihia namba ya tūtambi.

English: Additionally, selecting disease-resistant potato varieties and practicing crop rotation help mitigate the risk of diseases, ensuring a healthier crop.
Kikuyu: Harī oūguo rī, ūthuri wa mbeu īretiria mīrīmū, kūhada mīmera ngūrani mūgūda o kīmera ni gūteithagia kūnyihia gūthegea kwa mūrimū na ūgūo kūgīa mīmera mīega

English: What are the tips for disease management?
Kikuyu: Nī hitho īrīkū cīa kūgīrīrīria mīrīmū?

English: Use recommended pesticides Use tolerant and resistant varieties.
Kikuyu: Hūthīra dawa cia tūtambi īria ciedekithitio na kūhada mītheba ya mīmera īrīa īngītiria mīrimū.

English: Practise crop protection.
Kikuyu: Menyagīrīea mīmera yaku

English: Plant healthy and certified tubers.
Kikuyu: Hada mbeū iria ciedekithitio

English: Ensure field sanitation.
Kikuyu:
Tīgīrīra mūgūnda waku nī
 mutheru

English: Ensure timely and proper weed control.
Kikuyu: Rīmīra mūgūnda īhīnda rīrīa rībatiī

English: Ensure adequate spacing.
Kikuyu: Tīgīthūkanīa wega mīmera yakū

English: Plant early.
Kikuyu: Hada gatene

English: Ensure proper crop nutrition
Kikuyu:
Tigīrīra niwahe mīmera īrio irīa ībatiī
 nigūo gūkūra wega

English: How are fungal diseases controlled in potatoes at the time of planting?
Kikuyu: Mīrīmū ya fungī waruini īngīgirirīrio atia mahida ma kūhada

English: To control fungal diseases on seed tubers, they should be dipped in a solution containing 2 to 3 g fungicide/L of water prior to any sunlight treatment;
Kikuyu: Nīgīo kūgirīrīria mīrīmū ya fungal waruini, wagīrīrīrio kurīnda waru cīaku dawaini ya 2-3g harī rita 20 cia maaī, mbere ya kūrigita na rīua

English: If cut tubers are used, they should follow the given treatment methods:
Kikuyu: Koruo ni waru ūrahūthika, fuatīīrīra mītaratara īrīa īheyanītuo.

English: When cutting, the knife or sickle should be sterilized between every cut by dipping in hard alcohol or a fungicide solution.
Kikuyu: Ukīrenga rī, tigīrīra nīwathabia kahīu na kang'eri gaku wega na dawa ya kūūraga jamuthi.

English: Otherwise, the cut seed tuber can easily spread diseases to other tubers.
Kikuyu: Warū urīa mūteme no ūtambie mūrimū narūa kūrī waru iria ingīa.

English: After cutting, seed tubers should either be rubbed with ashes or Bordeaux mixture to avoid infection of the freshly cut part,and placed in the shade for 2 to 3 days.
Kikuyu: Waru ūngītemwo rī, ūbatiī kūhakwo mūhu kana mūtūkanio wa Bordeaux nigūo kūgirīrīria gūtaba kwa mūrimū kūrī warū ūngī na thutha ucio kūiguo hadū hena kīruru harī thikū 2-3.

English: What are some of the diseases that affect potato production in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīrimū īrīkū ūgīragīrīria ūkūria wa waru Kenya

English: Potato Mosaic Virus X, S and Y;Potato Leaf Roll Virus;Bacterial Wilt;Fusarium dry rot;Stem canker;Early blight;Late blight;
Kikuyu: Mīrīmū īno nī hamwe na, Potato Mosaic Virus X, S and Y;Potato Leaf Roll Virus;Bacterial Wilt;Fusarium dry rot;Stem canker;Early blight;Late blight;

English: What are the symptoms of bacterial wilt?
Kikuyu: Ni darīrī īrīkū cia Bacteria wilt?

English: Plants display sudden wilting and drying in the field even during wet periods.
Kikuyu: Mīmera nī īhothaga na īgathira o na hīndī īrīa kūrī na mbura.

English: In rapid disease development, entire plants wilt quickly without yellowing.
Kikuyu: Mīrimū īngīthiī na mbere na ihenya, mīmera yothe nī īūragaga na ihenya ītarī na rangi wa rangi wa ndathi.

English: Sections of diseased plants may wilt completely and dry up, while the remainder of the plant appears healthy;
Kikuyu: Icunjī cia mīmera īrīa īrī na mīrimū no ithūke biūna ciūmagare hīndī īrīa mīmera īrīa īngī īkoragwo īrī mīega;

English: Leaves become yellow at their bases, then the whole plant wilts and dies.
Kikuyu: Mathangū magatuīka ma rangi wa ndathi mūthingi-inī wamo, na thutha ūcio mūtī wothe ūgaitīka na ūkue.

English: When stems are cut a brown coloured ring will be visible.
Kikuyu: Hīndī īrīa mīmera ītinatio, mūbira wa rangi wa burauni nī ūkuoneka

English: When a tuber is cut in half, black or brown rings will be visible.
Kikuyu: Rīrīa ngigī īrarutwo icunjī igīrī, nī ūkuona mībira ya rangi mweru kana wa burauni.

English: If left for a while or squeezed, these rings will exude a thick white fluid.
Kikuyu: Tūngīmatiganīria kwa ihinda kana tūmahingīrīrie, tūhengereta tūu no tūtheremage kīndū kīrūmu kīerū.

English: Fluid exudes from tuber eyes, which results in soil sticking to tuber eyes when crops are harvested.
Kikuyu: maaī marīa maumaga maithoini ma waru nīmatūmaga tīīrī wīnyitīīrīre nagūo doro īkagia.

English: What are the symptoms of blackleg/soft rot in potato production?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū monanagia atī waru nī irūaga na ikagīa na kīronda?

English: The disease begins with yellowing and upward rolling of leaflets which eventually wilt and die.
Kikuyu: Mūrimū ūcio wambīrīria na kūgīa na mathangū ma rangi wa ndathi na magathiī na igūrū, na thutha-inī magathiī kūbutha na gūkua.

English: What are the symptoms of Potato Leafroll Virus
Kikuyu: Hihi nī maūndū marīkū monanagia Mūrimū wa Potato Leafroll Virus

English: In primary infections, symptoms first appear at the top of the plant where the leaves roll inwards and turn pale yellow, some may develop yellow margins.
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa mīrimū ya mbere, mīrimū īonekaga kīambīrīria-inī kīa mūtī ūcio harīa mathangū mathiaga na thīinī na magatuīka ma rangi wa ndathi, mamwe no makūre na mathangū ma rangi wa ndathi.

English: In secondary infections, the entire plant is affected all the leaves roll inwards, especially at the base of the plant.
Kikuyu: Thīinī wa mīrimū īngī ya thutha-inī, Mūtī wothe nī ūhutagia mahūa mothe magathiī na thīinī, na makīria mūthingi-inī wa mūtī.

English: Symptoms appear 2 to 3 weeks after crop emergence.
Kikuyu: Mīrimū īyo yonekaga thutha wa ciumia igīrī kana ithatū kuuma rīrīa magetha maambīrīirie kuoneka.

English: How can I store harvested potatoes to ensure their longevity?
Kikuyu: Ingīiga waru iria ciarutītwo atīa nīguo itūūre ihinda iraya?

English: Proper storage is vital for preserving the quality of harvested potatoes.
Kikuyu: Kūiga irio wega nī kwa bata gūtigīrīra atī indo iria ithondeketwo waru iria igethetwo.

English: Store potatoes in a cool, dark place with good ventilation to prevent sprouting and greening.
Kikuyu: Iga waru ciaku hadū hahehu, hatarī ūtheri na haria rīera rīracera wega nigūo kugīrīrīria kuma nguha na gecejia rangi wa grini.

English: Avoid storing them near onions, as both release gases that can accelerate spoilage.
Kikuyu: Wītheme kūiga waru hamwe na itūngūrū tondū nīīrutakaga rīera rīrīa rīcungagīrīrīa gūthuka

English: Regularly inspect stored potatoes, removing any showing signs of decay.
Kikuyu: Thūrimaga kaingi waru ciaku iria wīigite nīguo kūeheria iria mbuthu

English: Following these guidelines ensures your harvest stays fresh for an extended period.
Kikuyu: ūgīfuatīīrīra mītaratara īno rī, tīgīrīra magetha maku nimathaitīre wega gwa kahinda karaya

English: What are some of the value addition strategies in potato seed production?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra irīkū cingīhūthīka kuongerera ūciarithanio wa mbegū cia waru.

English: Value Addition Techniques in potato production include Cleaning, Sorting, Grading, & Processing
Kikuyu: Njīra cia kuongerera ūciarithanio wa waru ni hamwe na, guthambia, guthura, kūgredī na gūthīa.

English: What are the possible value addition options for irish potatoes?
Kikuyu: Nī njīra īrīkū cīa kuongerera ūgacīru wa waru.

English: The extent of potato development and the benefits derived for potato producers will depend directly on the conditions of the export and in-country market
Kikuyu: Wega wa ūkūria na ūtheremu wa ūkūria harī aciarithanī a waru nī īrīnganaga na thoko cia naja na cia būrūri.

English: When is the highest post harvest losses in potato experienced?
Kikuyu: Nīrī kūrī hathara makīria thutha wa magetha ma waru?

English: The hotspots for potato Post-Harvest Losses (PHL) are during harvesting, sorting/grading, and storage
Kikuyu: Maundu marīa macungagīrīria hadhara nyingī thutha wa magetha ma waru ni kūgetha, gūthūra, na kūiga waru

English: What are the Potato varieties in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya waru īrī Kenya?

English: Kenya offers a range of potato varieties, each with its unique characteristics.
Kikuyu: Kenya īkoragwo na mīthemba mīingī ya waru, o īmwe na ngumo yayo ya mwanya.

English: Varieties like Shangi, Dutch Robjin, and Tigoni are well-suited to different regions and climatic conditions.
Kikuyu: Mīthemba ta Shangi, Dutch Robjin, na Tigoni nī īhūthagīrūo kūndū kūingī na kūndū kwi rīera ngūrani

English: The registered number of potato varieties in Kenya stands at 56 and is increasing as more players come into the market.
Kikuyu: Namba ya mīthemba ya waru īrīa īandīkītwo Kenya nī 56 na nī īrathiī īkīongererekaga o ūrīa andū aingī marokaga thoko-inī īyo.

English: What potato varieties are best suited for farming in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya waru īrīa yagīrīire mūno kūrīma būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: In Kenya, several potato varieties thrive in different regions and climates.
Kikuyu: kenya rī mīthemba mīīngī ya waru īkuraga kūndū kūingī na kwīrīera ngūranī

English: Popular choices include Shangi, Dutch Robin, and Tigoni. Shangi is well-suited to higher altitudes, while Dutch Robjin and Tigoni perform excellently in various regions.
Kikuyu: Iria ikoragwo na ngumo nī ta Shangi, Dutch Robin, na Tigoni. Shangi nī īhūthagīrūo kūndū kūrīa kūraihu mūno, nayo Dutch Robin na Tigoni nīciakaga kūndū ngūrani.

English: It's crucial to select varieties based on your specific location and altitude to optimize yield.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū wa bata gūthuura mīthemba īringaīne na kūrīa ūikaraga na ūriku nīguo ūhote kūgīa na maciaro maingī.

English: What are the features of asante potato variety?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū magwetetwo megiī mīthemba ya waru ya Asante?

English: The Asante potato variety yields 14,000-18,000 kg per īka.
Kikuyu: Mūtararīko wa waru ya Asante ūciaraga kilo 14,000-18,000 harī acre īmwe.

English: It matures in 3-4 months from planting.
Kikuyu: īkūraga thutha wa mīeri ītatū nginya īna kuuma rīrīa yahandwo.

English: Its tubers are round with smooth pink skin.
Kikuyu: Mīmera yayo nī ya kībungo na īrī na rangi wa gakarakū.

English: Its optimal growing region is areas between 1,800-2,600 meters ASL.
Kikuyu: Mūgūnda ūrīa mwega mūno wa kūhanda nī kūndū kūrī na ūraihu wa mita 1,800-2,600 igūrū rīa thī.

English: The stems are medium, and its tubers have a high dry matter content.
Kikuyu: Mītī īyo nī ya gatagatī, na mīri īkoragwo na kīndū kīūmū mūno.

English: It is best for making chips, mashing, roasting and baking. This seed is tolerant of late blight disease.
Kikuyu: Nī mwega mūno harī gūthondeka chips, gūkīma, gūcinwo na kūruga. Mbeū īyo nī īhotaga kūhiūrania na mūrimū wa mbaa

English: What are the features of annet potato variety?
Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū cia mīthemba ya waru ya annet?

English: Annet variety grown in Medium to high altitude of 1300-2000 M.A.S.L.
Kikuyu: Mūtararīko wa Annet ūrīa ūkūragwo kūndū kūrīa kūraihu mūno ta mita 1300-2000 igūrū rīa thī.

English: It does well in areas such as Nyandarua, Kiambu, Nyeri, Laikipia, Meru, Nakuru, Bomet, Narok, Kwale, Nandi, Kisii, Kericho, Elgeyo Marakwet, Uasin Gishu, Trans-Nzoia, West Pokot, Taita Taveta and Cherangani hills.
Kikuyu: īkoragwo na ūgima mwega icigo-inī ta Nyandarua, Kiambu, Nyeri, Laikipia, Meru, Nakuru, Bomet, Narok, Kwale, Nandi, Kisii, Kericho, Elgeyo Marakwet, Uasin Gishu, Trans-Nzoia, West Pokot, Taita Taveta na irīma-inī cia Cherangani.

Kikuyu: Ngumo cia mĩmera:  Ĩkoragwo na mũhianĩre wa kĩbungo, maitho mariku, ngothi ya kĩrũrũ nyoroku, (na irema cia rangi mūtune), Rangi wa ngoikoni harī waru mweru. Kwa ihinda iraya (mieri ĩĩrĩ).

Kikuyu: Mūtheba wa Annet ūkūraga ta ūraihu wa mita īmwe(1m) wīna mīkorī īna hinya na mathangū ma grini hūthū ya thaithi ya gatagatīini.

Kikuyu: Kūhiūrania na mīrimū: Annet nī īhotaga kūhiūrania na mūrimū wa mbaa Mūrīmi wa mbegū: KALRO Tigoni.

Kikuyu: Nī ngumo irīkū cia mīthemba ya waru ya Arizona?

Kikuyu: Mūtararīko wa Arizona nī wagīrīire kūndū guothe kūrīa gūkūraga waru na ningī no ūhote gūkūra irīma-inī cia 1300- 3000.

Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa gūkūra: Kīambīrīria (16.19 tons/acre).

Kikuyu: Mūrīmi wa mbegū: Agrico Potato Services Africa Limited.

Kikuyu: Ngumo cia mīmera: Mīmera ya gatagatī, Maitho ma rangi wa pink, Ngūrū ya rangi wa yellow, Nyama ya rangi wa yellow

Kikuyu: īkoragwo na ūgima mwega icigo-inī ta Nyandarua, Kiambu, Nyeri, Laikipia, Meru, Nakuru, Bomet, Narok, Kwale, Nandi, Kisii, Kericho, Elgeyo Marakwet, Uasin Gishu,Trans-Nzoia, West Pokot, Taita-Taveta, na irīma-inī cia Cherangani.

Kikuyu: Kīhumo kīa mūhiano wa thahabu ya bururu kīarī KALRO mwaka wa 2010.

Kikuyu: Nī mwega gūkīma, gūtherūkia, gūcina na gūthondeka chips.

Kikuyu: Mūtararīko wa waru wa Kerr ūrīa ūīkaine o na wītagwo pink njērū

Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mūndū agīrīirūo nī gwīciria akīenda gūthuura mīthemba ya waru?

English: Select varieties adapted to local climatic conditions to ensure wide adaptability and stable production.
Kikuyu: Gūthuura mīthemba īrīa yagīrīire kūringana na rīera rīa kūu nīguo īkorūo na ūhoti mūnene wa kūgarūrīra na kūgīa na magetha ma gūtūūra.

English: Abandon varieties with poor storage characteristics and low levels of resistance to major diseases.
Kikuyu: Tigana na mīthemba īrīa ītarī njega kūigwo na ītarī na ūhoti mūnene wa kūhiūrania na mīrimū mīnene.

English: Promote varieties that are already grown in the country and are accepted by farmers and commercial markets.
Kikuyu: Gūtūūria mīthemba īrīa īramūrīkwo būrūri-inī na īkamenyeka nī arīmi na biacara.

English: Direct sunlight on potato seed should be avoided.
Kikuyu: Ndwagīrīirūo kūhūūra mbegū cia waru na riūa.

English: What are ware potatoes?
Kikuyu: Waru wa ware nī kīī ?

English: ware potato means potatoes produced, graded and marketed for human consumption or industrial use.
Kikuyu: Waru ya kũruga nĩ waru ūria ūciarithanitio, ugathuruo na ĩgũtwarwo thoko-inĩ nĩ ũndũ wa kũhũthĩrwo nĩ andũ kana kũhũthĩrũo  igandaini.

English: What are seed potatoes?
Kikuyu: Waru cia kūhanda nī kīī?

English: seed potatoes means potatoes produced and traded for propagation use
Kikuyu: mbembe cia kūhanda nī mbembe iria ithondekagwo na igatwarwo thoko-inī nī ūndū wa kūciarīthanīa

English: What are the standard package for ware potatoes?
Kikuyu: Nī indo irīkū cia mūhianīre cia kūrūgamīrīra waru cia kūruga?

English: The standard weight for a bag or package of ware potatoes shall be one hundred and ten kilogramme with a tolerance of pltrs or minus five kilogrammes
Kikuyu: ūritū wa mūhianīre wa ngunia īmwe kana kībungo kīmwe kīa waru nī igana rīmwe na ikūmi na ūritū wa kuongerera kana kweheria ithano

English: What are some of the potato seed varieties in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Nī mīthemba īrīkū ya mbegu cia waru īrī Kenya?

English: Some of the seed varieties available are: Kenya Mavuno, Rudolph, Shangi, Sherekea, and Tigoni. Others include Ambition, Arizona, Arnova, Kenya Karibu, Dutch Robin among others.
Kikuyu: Imwe cia mbegū iria ikoragwo kuo nī: Kenya Mavuno, Rudolph, Shangi, Sherekea, na Tigoni. Angī nī Ambition, Arizona, Arnova, Kenya Karibu, Dutch Robin na ingī.

English: Where can certified seeds be got in Kenya?
Kikuyu: Mbegū iria ciīkīrītwo irūndo igīoneka kū būrūri-inī wa Kenya?

English: It is recommended to plant certified seeds from official sources e.g. Agricultural Development Corporation (ADC), to prevent diseases such as bacterial wilt and early blight.
Kikuyu: Nī wega kūhanda mbegū iria ciīhokekete kuuma kūrī andū a thirikari, ta: Agricultural Development Corporation (ADC), kūgirīrīria mīrimū ta kūbutha na kūhīa tene.

English: There are also certified sources.
Kikuyu: Ningī nī kūrī na indo ingī cia gwītīkīrīka.

English: How to choose the best potato seed?
Kikuyu: ūngīhota atīa gūthuura mbegū njega cia waru?

English: The top factors to consider when choosing the best potato varieties for the highest yields and income are; Climatic conditions of your region.
Kikuyu: Maūndū marīa magīrīire gwīcirio rīrīa ūgūthuura mīthemba īrīa njega ya waru nī ūndū wa magetha maingī na mbeca nyingī nī ta: Mītūūrīre ya rīera rīa kūrīa ūikaraga.

English: Some potato varieties thrive in cooler highland areas, while others are more suitable for warmer lowland regions.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īmwe ya mbembe nī īkenagīra mūno kūndū kūrīa na heho, nayo īngī īkenagīra mūno kūndū kūrīa na ūrugarī.

English: Understanding the specific temperature and rainfall requirements of different potato varieties is essential for successful cultivation.
Kikuyu: Nī ūndū wa bata kūmenya ūrīa mīthemba ngūrani ya waru ībataranagia harī ūrugarī na mbura nīguo mūndū ahote kūrima wega.

English: to choose this consider the Altitude range in meters above sea level for each seed.
Kikuyu: nīguo ūhote gūthuura ūndū ūcio, wīcirie igūrū rīa ūraihu igūrū rīa iria harī mbegū o yothe.

English: Some potato varieties are ideal for boiling, roasting, or making chips, or processing into flour or crisps.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īmwe ya mbembe nī njega mūno ya gūtherūkia, gūcina, kana gūthondeka chips, kana gūthondeka mūtu kana crips.

English: Grow a variety with a high market demand and your specific target market will greatly influence your profitability.
Kikuyu: Gūkūria mīthemba īrīa īkoragwo na bata mūnene wa indo cia thoko na thoko īrīa ūgūcaria nī īgūgūteithia kūgīa na mbeca.

English: The tuber skin and flesh color may also be a consideration in this.
Kikuyu: Rangi wa ngothi na nyama no ūkorūo ūrī ūndū ūngīciria ūhoro waguo.

English: Resistance to diseases and pests:Certain potato varieties have higher resistance to common diseases such as late blight, bacterial wilt, or potato cyst nematodes.
Kikuyu: Kūregana na mīrimū na igunyū: Mīmera īmwe ya waru nī īkoragwo na ūhoti mūnene wa kūhiūrania na mīrimū mīingī, ta mūrimū wa kūbutha, kūbutha nī bakteria, kana kūbutha nī tūbungo twa macungwa.

English: By choosing resistant varieties, farmers can minimize the need for chemical interventions and increase the chances of a healthy crop.
Kikuyu: Arīmi mangīthuura mīmera īrīa ītangīūraga mīrimū īyo, no mahūthīre indo itiganīte na kwoguo magīe na mbegū njega.

English: Maturity period choose a potato variety with a short number of days it takes to mature from planting.
Kikuyu: Ihinda rīa kūhīa rīagīrīire gūthuurūo na njīra ya kūhūthīra matukū manini kuuma hīndī ya kūhanda nginya hīndī ya gūkūra.

English: it will give back your investments in a shorter time Potential yield per īka;
Kikuyu: no īgūcokeria mbeca ciaku na ihenya mūno.

English: Whether you are growing potato for food or sale, choose a potato with higher yields per ton/ha or ton/īka.
Kikuyu: O na angīkorūo ūrarima waru nī ūndū wa irio kana nī ūndū wa kwendia, thuura waru īrīa īrī na magetha maingī harī tani harī hekarū kana tani harī hekarū.

English: Tuber dormancy; If you want to store tubers over an extended period, choose a variety with a longer tuber dormancy,
Kikuyu: ūgima wa mīmera: ūngīenda kūiga mīmera ihinda iraya, thuura mīthemba īrīa īkoragwo na ihinda iraya ya gūkoma.

English: It will ensure you have a fresh supply throughout the year.
Kikuyu: ūndū ūcio nī ūrīgūteithagia kūgīa na indo njerū mwaka wothe.

English: What are the factors to consider when selecting site for multiple variety potato seed production?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū magīrīire gwīcirio hīndī ya gūthuura mūgūnda wa kūhanda mbegū cia mīthemba mīingī ya waru?

English: Just plant one variety in the field or, in case of more varieties, have them properly separated for easy management.
Kikuyu: Handaga mūgūnda-inī mūmera wa mūthemba ūmwe tu, kana ūngīkorūo ūrī na mīthemba mīingī, ītigithūranīrie wega nīguo ūhote kūramata.

English: Different varieties may mature at different times, or may need different agronomic management.
Kikuyu: Mīmera ngūrani no īre ihinda rītiganīte, kana ībatare kūmenyererio na njīra ngūrani.

English: Note that there is no risk of cross pollination in potatoes.
Kikuyu: Ririkana atī gūtirī ūgwati Kūhūūrana na mīmera kūngīhotithirie mbembe kūingīha.

English: What are the advantages of certified seeds in potato seed production?
Kikuyu: Nī ūguni ūrīkū ūkoragwo na mbegū iria ciīrīkītio harī kūhanda mbegū cia waru?

English: Better yield, pest/disease resistance and drought tolerance.
Kikuyu: Mūingīhe, ūhoti wa kūrūa na mīrimū na ūhoti wa na kūrūa na ng'aragu.

English: Certified seeds are a product of many years of research and development combined with excellent agronomic practices.
Kikuyu: Mbegū cia kūhanda ciūmanagā na Mīaka mīingī ya ūthuthuria na ūtuīria ītukithanītio na mītugo mīega ya ūrīmi.

English: Certified seeds have good attributes, are healthy, strong and well adapted for different agro ecological zones.
Kikuyu: Mbegū iria ithomithītio nī njega, irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī, na nī ciagīrīire kūhūthīrūo mīgūnda-inī ngūrani.

English: Seed potato is disease free since regular inspections are done in the seed potato fields by well-trained government officials.
Kikuyu: Mbegũ cia mĩmera itirĩ na mĩrimũ tondũ thirikari nĩ ĩmenyerete mũno wĩra ũcio wa gũthuthuria mĩgũnda ya waru.

English: Seed potato production is traceable and hence brings accountability.
Kikuyu: ūrīmi wa waru nī ūmenyekaga na kwoguo nī ūrehagīra andū wīra.

English: Seed potato maintains the value traits of the crop.
Kikuyu: Ngarīki ya mbegū īgūtūūria ngumo cia bata cia mbegū īcio.

English: Many good traits of a potato variety can be gotten in certified potato seed.
Kikuyu: Ngumo nyingī njega cia mīthemba ya waru no cionekage thīinī wa mbegū cia mbembe iria ithomithītio.

English: Certified seed potato compliments your good agronomic practices making sure you get the most from your inputs, it saves cost of inputs since disease and pest incidence is lower.
Kikuyu: Mbegū cia kūhanda iria ciīna kībaū nī cia kūgaathīrīria mītugo yaku mīega ya ūrīmi kūgīa na ma atī nī ūragunīka biū kuumana na indo iria ūhūthīrīte, nī kūhonokagia thogora wa indo iria ūhūthīrīte tondū mūrimū na tūnyamū tūrīa tūrehaga mīrimū nī tūnyihīte.

English: What are the features of quality seed tubers?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū makonainie na mbegū cia mbeū njega?

English: Free from seed-borne disease and pests.
Kikuyu: ītarī na mīrimū na tūnyamū tūngī tūrehagwo nī mbeū.

English: Seed tubers must not be mixed with other varieties.
Kikuyu: Mbeu cia mbegū itiagīrīire kūnyitithanīrio na mīthemba īngī.

English: High sprouting vigour.
Kikuyu: Kūgīa na hinya mūingī wa kūhanda.

English: Seed tuber weight should be 30 to 50 g to reduce the need for cutting that may introduce infection.
Kikuyu: ūritū wa mbegū cia mīmera nī wagīrīirūo gūkorūo ūrī 30 nginya 50 g nīguo ūhote kūnyihanyihia bata wa kūiga tūcunjī twa mbegū kūrehe mūrimū.

English: Why is sunlight treatment in seed tubers?
Kikuyu: Nī kīī gītūmaga mīmera ya mbegū īgīe na ūtheri wa riūa?

English: A sunlight treatment involves exposing seed tubers to sunlight for 20 to 30 days at 10 to 20º C before planting to stimulate healthy and uniform sprouts (4 to 8) on each of the tubers.
Kikuyu: Kūhīa na riūa nī kuuga kūhūūra mīmera ya mbegū riūa-inī kwa mīthenya 20 nginya 30 harī 10o C nginya 20o C mbere ya kūhanda nīguo mīmera īgīe na mīmera mīega na īkoragwo īiganaine (4 nginya 8) harī o mūmera.

English: Seed tubers with 4 to 8 young sprouts emerges from every eye, which are 0.5 to 1 cm long, strong, healthy, and green are best for planting.
Kikuyu: Maciaro ma mbegū marī na tūcungwa 4 nginya 8 tūrīa tūrī na ūraihu wa sentimita 0.5 nginya 1, tūrī na hinya na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī, na mīmera īrīa mīruru nī mīega ya kūhanda.

English: What is the recommended potato seed is for planting?
Kikuyu: Nī mbegū irīkū cia waru cia kūhanda irutagwo?

English: A grower should use seed potato tubers that are healthy, strong, have good colour, and have broken dormancy with 4 to 6 sprouts per tuber
Kikuyu: Mūrīmi agīrīirūo kūhūthīra mbegū cia mbembe iria irī na ūgima mwega wa mwīrī, irī na hinya, irī na rangi mwega, na irī na mīmera 4 nginya 6 harī o mbegū

English: What are some of the factors to consider in potato seed production preparation?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū magīrīire gwīcirio rīrīa mūndū arabanga mbegū cia kūhanda?

English: Buy clean seed that has been certified by KEPHIS.
Kikuyu: ūgūre mbegū theru iria ciīkīrītwo nī KEPHIS.

English: When collecting the seeds from the seed merchant Agrico PSA, the farmer is advised not to store the seed for more than 5 days.
Kikuyu: Arīmi magīthuura mbegū kuuma kūrī Agrico PSA, nī magīrīirwo kwaga gūciiga makīria ma thikū ithano.

English: Potatoes should not be stored in a space where other potatoes are kept, where other foodstocks, chemicals or fertilizers are kept.
Kikuyu: Mbatata itiagīrīirūo kūigwo handū harīa ingī ciigītwo, kūrīa irio ingī, dawa ya kūhūra mūgūnda kana mboga ingī ciigītwo.

English: The storage space needs to be disinfected prior to use.If potatoes are stored temporarily
Kikuyu: Kūiga indo icio nī kūbataraga gūtherio na kīndū gīa gūtheria mīrimū mbere ya kūhūthīrūo.

English: It should be in a dry, dark, very well-ventilated place where temperatures don’t go below 10 degrees or above 20 degrees, and the potatoes can not be affected by rodents or insects, or other elements of nature.
Kikuyu: No mūhaka akorūo arī handūharīa hatarī maaī, handū harī ndūma, harī rīera rīega, kūrīa rīera rītikoragwo na ūrugarī wa digrii 10 kana makīria ma digrii 20, na kūrīa waru itangīthūkagio nī tūnyamū kana tūnyamū tūngī kana indo ingī cia ndūire.

English: Be careful when transporting your seeds.
Kikuyu: Wīmenyerere rīrīa ūratwara mbeū ciaku.

English: Do not transport potato seeds in an open truck.
Kikuyu: Ndūgatwarage mbegū cia waru na rori ītarī na gīkūniko

English: If it rains and the potatoes get wet, they can start rotting within 24 hours.
Kikuyu: Kūgiūra mbura na waru inyitwo nī maaī, no ciambīrīrie kūbutha thutha wa mathaa 24.

English: Potatoes should not be transported in a truck together with any other products, especially with ripening fruits.
Kikuyu: Waru itiagīrīirūo gūtwarwo na ngari hamwe na indo ingī, na makīria matunda marīa marīkītie kwirūa

English: Fruits emit high levels of ethylene, which can damage the sprouts and stop the potatoes from germinating.
Kikuyu: Matūnda nī marekagiriria ethylene nyingī mūno, na ūndū ūcio no ūthūkie mīmera īyo na ūgirie mbatatī īciarage.

English: Potatoes should not be transported in a truck that carries or has carried fruits a day before.
Kikuyu: Waru itiagīrīirūo gūtwarwo na rori īratwarithia kana īratwarithītie matunda mūthenya ūcio ūrathirire.

English: If that is the case, make sure the truck is cleaned and keep the doors open for a long time to reduce the level of ethylene prior to loading
Kikuyu: Angīkorūo nīguo-rī, ririkana atī rori nī ītheretio na ūtigīre mīrango īrī mīhingūre kwa ihinda iraya nīguo ūnyihanyihie ethylene mbere ya kūbokerwo

English: Why is sorting important as a value addition strategy?
Kikuyu: Nīkī gūthuura nī ūndū wa bata harī gwīkīra indo cia bata?

English: Diseased and cut tubers are sorted out to avoid losses in storage due to rotting
Kikuyu: Mīmera īrīa īrī na mīrimū na īrīa ītinangītwo nī īthuuragwo wega nīguo ītigakorūo īthirīte nī ūndū wa kūbutha

English: What are some of the factors to consider in potato grading?
Kikuyu: Nī maūndū marīkū mangībatara gwīcūrania rīrīa tūratua ngūrani cia waru?

English: Potatoes are graded depending on size and shape of tuber;
Kikuyu: Waru ni īthūragwo kūringana na mūigana na mūhianīre.

English: Malformed tubers are removed;Ware: beyond 60 mm gauge;Seed: 28 – 60mm gauge;Charts: Less than 28 mm gauge
Kikuyu: Mīmera īrīa ītarī mīgima nī īrutagwo;Mbeū: makīria ma 60 mm;Mbeū: 28 ⁇ 60 mm; Tūbiri: Itarī ikinyītie 28 mm

English: How should ware potatoes be stored?
Kikuyu: Mīmera īyo īgīrīire kūigwo atīa?

English: Ware tubers should be kept in a dark store to prevent greening.
Kikuyu: Mīmera īyo yagīrīirwo kūigwo handū hatarī na ūtheri nīguo ītigathūke.

English: The store should be cool and well ventilated;
Kikuyu: Nyūmba īyo yagīrīirūo gūkorūo īrī theru na īgakorūo na rīera rīega.

English: How should seed potato be stored?
Kikuyu: Mbegū cia kūhanda ciagīrīirūo kūigwo na njīra īrīkū?

English: Seed potatoes are kept in a cool store with diffuse light for coloured, short sprouts to develop, however, avoid direct sunlight
Kikuyu: Mbegū cia waru ciigagwo handū hega hatarī na ūtheri mūingī nīgetha ikūre na ngūrū na irī na marangi, no ndūgaikare ūtheri-inī wa riū
